title: User Guide
text:
PRISM is a comprehensive machine learning toolkit designed to advance auditory scene analysis (Ecological Momentary Assessment) for hearing devices. This repository contains the code, models, and tools developed for the “Australian Future Hearing Initiative”.
PRISM helps researchers collect real-world data (“catch you in the moment”) through the smartphone application. When a participant interacts with the PRISM app, we capture a snapshot of your real-world environment. This includes: - Audio: A short 30-second clip of the sound around you. - Context: Your rating of how hard it was to hear speech.
This data trains a Machine Learning models to automatically recognize difficult listening situations and adjust hearing aids in real-time.
The PRISM web frontend serves as the interface for data collection and study management for Researchers conducting “experiments” within the PRISM ecosystem. Researchers use this application to design experiments, manage study cohorts, and deploy tasks to participants. It is the control center for your data collection needs.

title: PRISM
text:
PRISM is a comprehensive machine learning toolkit designed to advance auditory scene analysis (Ecological Momentary Assessment) for hearing devices. This repository contains the code, models, and tools developed for the “Australian Future Hearing Initiative”.
PRISM might stand for:
Modern hearing aids often struggle to adapt to rapidly changing sound environments—like moving from a quiet room to a busy street or a noisy restaurant. This technology bridges the gap between raw audio processing and intelligent environmental awareness, leading to a more seamless and personalised hearing experience for users.

title: PRISM
text:
Capabilities

Benchmark New Models: Use the standardized ahead_ds split to compare your model’s performance against the OpenYAMNet baseline.
Fine-Tuning: Leverage pre-trained YAMNet+ weights to adapt the model to new acoustic domains with minimal data.
Reproducibility: Full scripts for dataset generation (prism-ml-dataset-ahead-ds) and model training (prism-ml-yamnetp-tune) are provided.

title: PRISM
text:
Citation
If you use this code, model, or dataset in your research, please cite:
@misc{zhong2026datasetmodelauditoryscene,
      title={A dataset and model for auditory scene recognition for hearing devices: AHEAD-DS and OpenYAMNet}, 
      author={Henry Zhong and Jörg M. Buchholz and Julian Maclaren and Simon Carlile and Richard Lyon},
      year={2026},
      eprint={2508.10360},
      archivePrefix={arXiv},
      primaryClass={cs.SD},
      url={https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.10360}, 
}

title: Developer Guide
text: The PRISM Ecosystem consists of a backend server built with FastAPI and a web frontend built with Flutter. Both rely on Firebase for authentication and database storage. This guide provides instructions on how to set up the PRISM system locally for development or deployment.

title: Developer Guide
text: The PRISM Ecosystem consists of a backend server built with FastAPI and a web frontend built with Flutter. Both rely on Firebase for authentication and database storage. This guide provides instructions on how to set up the PRISM system locally for development or deployment.

title: Developer Guide
text:
Firebase
Get a paid project on Firebase and make a new project.

title: Developer Guide
text:
Unified Setup via Docker
The easiest way to install and run both the PRISM Backend and Frontend is via the prism-setup repository, which uses Docker Compose.
here!

1. Prerequisites (Do this first!)
Before running the installer, you must install the “engine” that powers the app.

Step A: Install Docker

Download Docker Desktop for your computer:

Download for Mac (Apple Chip/M1/M2)
Download for Mac (Intel Chip)
Download for Windows

Run the installer and follow the instructions.
IMPORTANT: Open the “Docker Desktop” app after installing it.

Wait until the whale icon in your status bar stops animating.
If it asks for permission to use your network/files, click Allow.




Step B: Install Git

Mac Users: You likely already have it. If not, open Terminal and type git --version. If it asks to install developer tools, say “Yes”.
Windows Users: Download Git Here.




Installation (Do this once)

Open your Terminal (Mac) or Command Prompt (Windows).
Clone this installer: ```bash git clone https://github.com/Australian-Future-Hearing-Initiative/prism-setup.git cd prism-setup
Run the installer script: Mac/Linux: sh install.sh.
Windows: You may need to run this in “Git Bash” or manually clone the repos.
Add your Keys: Drag your serviceAccountKey.json file into this prism-setup folder. Drag your .env file into this prism-setup folder.



🔐 Credential Setup Troubleshooting

1. Firebase Service Account Key
If serviceAccountKey.json is missing: 1. Go to Firebase Console > Project Settings (gear icon). 2. Click Service Accounts > Generate New Private Key. 3. Rename the file to serviceAccountKey.json and place it in the root directory.


2. Gmail App Password (for Invites)
Standard passwords won’t work. 1. Enable 2-Step Verification on your Google Account at https://myaccount.google.com/security 2. Search for “App Passwords”. 3. Generate a key for “Other (Custom Name)” and use that 16-character code in your .env.


3. JWT Secret
Generate a unique secret for your session tokens: openssl rand -hex 32


4. Firebase Web API Key
If you need the FIREBASE_API_KEY for your .env file:
Go to Project settings > General.
Scroll down to the “Your apps” section.
Select your web app (e.g., prism (web)).
In the “SDK setup and configuration” pane, look for the const firebaseConfig block.
Copy the value next to apiKey.


4. Run the installer script again:
Mac/Linux: sh install.sh.
Windows: You may need to run this in “Git Bash” or manually clone the repos.



How to Start the App
Whenever you want to run PRISM, just open this folder in terminal and type:
docker-compose up --build
Wait until it finishes, then:
Frontend Dashboard: http://localhost:3000 Backend API: http://localhost:8000
XXXXXXXXXXXXxEnd here!

title: Developer Guide
text:
Manual Frontend Setup
The frontend is a Flutter web application.
The PRISM web frontend serves as the interface for data collection and study management for Researchers conducting “experiments” within the PRISM ecosystem as part of the Australian Future Hearing Initiative.


For Researchers
Researchers use this application to design experiments, manage study cohorts, and deploy tasks to participants. It is the control center for your data collection needs.
How to use: 1. Login: Access the researcher dashboard securely. 2. Manage Projects: Create new projects to house specific studies or experiments. 3. Configure Experiments: * Set up various experiment types (e.g., Ecological Momentary Assessment (EMA) surveys, sensor-thresholds). * Define parameters and logical flows for the experiments. 4. Manage Participants: * Add new participants to the system. * Assign participants to specific projects/cohorts. * Monitor participant progress through assigned tasks. * Email participants. * Download audiograms
See the complete userguide: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_FZVBiN1QqhP50A2izS7KlW_Y021koJM45pSYcHEkMw/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.qbhdu5cvo4bq



Developer Instructions

1. Launch the Backend Server
This frontend relies on the prism-flutter-backend. See the repo for launch instructions.


2. Configure Environment
Create a .env file in the root directory and add the URL of the backedn server, e.g.:
## Base URL for API calls when running locally
API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8000


3. Install FVM (Flutter Version Management)
We use FVM to ensure consistent Flutter versions.

Option 1 Dart Pub: dart pub global activate fvm
Option 2 Homebrew: brew tap leoafarias/fvm && brew install fvm
Option 3 Install Script: curl -fsSL https://fvm.app/install.sh | bash



4. Run the App
Execute the following commands from the root of this repository:
fvm flutter channel stable
fvm flutter upgrade
fvm flutter pub get
## fvm dart run build_runner watch -d    # Optional: watch freezed/json files for changes
fvm flutter run -d chrome             # Run the app in Chrome


🧪 Testing & Architecture

Unit & Widget Tests: flutter test
Specific Test: flutter test <path/to/test.dart>
Integration Tests: bash     flutter drive --driver=test_driver/integration_test.dart --target=integration_test/participant_flow_test.dart -d chrome Note: Web integration tests inject bytes directly as WebDriver screens cannot handle drag-and-drop.
Coverage: flutter test --coverage

Key Directory Structure: * lib/ui: Contains all screens and widgets, separated by feature (e.g., experiment, participant, auth). * lib/domain: Business logic and models. * lib/data: Data repositories and API services.
